Home / Research Projects / Procurement Anomaly Detection

Procurement Anomaly Detection

Applied ML system for anomaly detection in public procurement data, combining unsupervised methods with explainability for civic transparency.

Anomaly DetectionExplainabilityCivic AIPublic Sector
View code

Overview

End-to-end pipeline for analysing public procurement data using Finnish open data from avoindata.fi. Combines Isolation Forest and Local Outlier Factor for anomaly detection with SHAP-based explainability and rule-based audit reports. Includes a Streamlit KPI dashboard and dbt-based reproducible transformations, aligned with EU Directive 2014/24/EU.

Methods

Isolation Forest, LOF, SHAP, rule-based auditing, dbt transformations.

Evaluation

Empirical evaluation on Finnish procurement data with audit-aligned thresholds.

Key Contributions

  • Isolation Forest and LOF anomaly detection with configurable contamination.
  • SHAP-based explainability and rule-based audit explanations.
  • Interactive KPI dashboard for vendor concentration and contract distributions.
  • Reproducible pipeline via dbt and Makefile orchestration.
  • Aligned with EU Directive 2014/24/EU for procurement transparency.

Research & Engineering Challenges

  • Tuning unsupervised thresholds to minimise false positives in audit settings.
  • Building interpretable SHAP explanations for non-technical auditors.
  • Adapting the pipeline to heterogeneous EU procurement data formats.

Tech Stack

Python Streamlit Scikit-learn Isolation Forest LOF SHAP dbt

Status

Completed

Author
